Current Ownership Structure YouTube is owned by Google, which is a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc. Alphabet Inc. is a multinational conglomerate created in 2015 as a result of Google’s corporate restructuring. Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin formed Alphabet to allow for autonomy in their various business ventures and to separate Google’s core internet business from its other innovative projects. Alphabet’s Role In YouTube’s Ownership As a wholly owned subsidiary of Google, YouTube is part of Alphabet Inc. Alphabet has complete ownership and control of YouTube’s operations, finances, and strategic decisions. Alphabet’s overarching objective is to manage and expand its diverse portfolio of businesses, of which YouTube is a significant component. Title: Who Owns YouTube?
Content: Current Ownership Structure
YouTube is owned by Google, which is a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc.
Alphabet Inc. is a multinational conglomerate created in 2015 as a result of Google’s corporate restructuring. Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin formed Alphabet to allow for autonomy in their various business ventures and to separate Google’s core internet business from its other innovative projects.
Alphabet’s Role In YouTube’s Ownership
As a wholly owned subsidiary of Google, YouTube is part of Alphabet Inc. Alphabet has complete ownership and control of YouTube’s operations, finances, and strategic decisions. Alphabet’s overarching objective is to manage and expand its diverse portfolio of businesses, of which YouTube is a significant component.

Title: Who Owns YouTube?
Content: The acquisition also brought YouTube a level of credibility and legitimacy, attracting more established content creators, media companies, and advertisers. Google’s extensive network of advertiser relationships and its advertising platforms, such as Google AdSense, helped monetize the platform effectively.
This enabled YouTube to offer revenue-sharing programs to content creators, allowing them to earn money from their videos and incentivizing high-quality content creation.
Google’s infrastructure and algorithms also helped improve the user experience on YouTube. Introducing features such as recommended videos and personalized playlists enhanced engagement and kept users returning to the platform. Google’s expertise in search and data analytics also played a critical role in improving video discoverability and ensuring that relevant content was surfaced to users.
Current Ownership Structure
YouTube is owned by Google, which is a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc.

Title: Who Owns YouTube?
Content: Role In YouTube’s Ownership As a wholly owned subsidiary of Google, YouTube is part of Alphabet Inc. Alphabet has complete ownership and control of YouTube’s operations, finances, and strategic decisions. Alphabet’s overarching objective is to manage and expand its diverse portfolio of businesses, of which YouTube is a significant component. Alphabet’s Subsidiaries and Structure Alphabet comprises several subsidiaries, each operating as an independent entity under Alphabet’s supervision. These subsidiaries, including Google, Nest, Waymo, and others, cover various industries and have their CEO responsible for their respective operations. How YouTube Fits Into Alphabet’s Broader Portfolio YouTube occupies a prominent position within Alphabet’s diverse portfolio of businesses. As a video-sharing platform, it has become an integral part of Google’s digital ecosystem, offering a vast library of user-generated content, music videos, and professional content from various media outlets.

Title: Who owns the YouTube company? | Replayed Blog
Content: Key stakeholders in YouTube's ownership
The ownership landscape of YouTube is multifaceted, with several key stakeholders playing significant roles in its operation and governance. At the forefront is Alphabet Inc., the parent company of Google, which oversees YouTube's strategic direction and financial performance. Alphabet's leadership, particularly its executives and board members, influence the broader goals for YouTube, ensuring that it aligns with the company's vision for digital advertising and content delivery. This connection provides YouTube with substantial resources and technological advancements, facilitating its continued growth and adaptability in a competitive market.

📃 Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/YouTube
Title: YouTube - Wikipedia
Content: On February 14, 2025, YouTube celebrated 20 years since its founding.
YouTube has been led by a CEO since its founding in 2005, beginning with Chad Hurley, who led the company until 2010. After Google's acquisition of YouTube, the CEO role was retained. Salar Kamangar took over Hurley's position and kept the job until 2014. He was replaced by Susan Wojcicki, who later resigned in 2023.[99] The current CEO is Neal Mohan, who was appointed on February 16, 2023.[99]

Title: YouTube - Wikipedia
Content: owned by Google. YouTube was founded on February 14, 2005, by Chad Hurley, Jawed Karim, and Steve Chen, who were former employees of PayPal. Headquartered in San Bruno, California, it is the second-most-visited website in the world, after Google Search. In January 2024, YouTube had more than 2.7 billion monthly active users, who collectively watched more than one billion hours of videos every day.[7] As of May 2019[update], videos were being uploaded to the platform at a rate of more than 500 hours of content per minute,[8][9] and as of mid-2024[update], there were approximately 14.8 billion videos in total.[10] On November 13, 2006, YouTube was purchased by Google for US$1.65 billion (equivalent to $2.39 billion in 2024).[11] Google expanded YouTube's business model of generating revenue from advertisements alone, to offering paid content such as movies and exclusive content produced by and for YouTube. It also offers YouTube Premium, a paid subscription option for watching content

Following public outrage over the unprecedented amount of unskippable ads, YouTube "ended" the experiment on September 19 of that year.[97] In October, YouTube announced that they would be rolling out customizable user handles in addition to channel names, which would also become channel URLs.[98] Recent history (2023–present) YouTube logo from August 2017 until February 5th, 2025 On February 16, 2023, Wojcicki announced that she would step down as CEO, with Neal Mohan named as her successor. Users of ad blockers are shown a message asking them to allow ads or inviting them to subscribe to the ad-free YouTube Premium subscription plan. Title: History of YouTube - Wikipedia
Content: From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia YouTube logo since October 22, 2024 Design history of the video player used by YouTube. Each layout generation might have gone through minor undocumented iterations, and that appearance may vary with embedding parameters. YouTube is an American online video-sharing platform headquartered in San Bruno, California, founded by three former PayPal employees—Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim—in February 2005. Google bought the site in November 2006 for US$1.65 billion, since which it operates as one of Google's subsidiaries. YouTube allows users to upload videos, view them, rate them with likes and dislikes, share them, add videos to playlists, report, make comments on videos, and subscribe to other users. The slogan "Broadcast Yourself" used for several years and the reference to user profiles as "Channels" signifies the premise upon which the platform is based, of allowing anyone to operate a personal broadcasting station in resemblance to

Content: In 2022, YouTube launched an experiment where the company would show users who watched longer videos on TVs a long chain of short unskippable adverts, intending to consolidate all ads into the beginning of a video. Following public outrage over the unprecedented amount of unskippable ads, YouTube "ended" the experiment on September 19 of that year.[97] In October, YouTube announced that they would be rolling out customizable user handles in addition to channel names, which would also become channel URLs.[98]
Recent history (2023–present)

On February 16, 2023, Wojcicki announced that she would step down as CEO, with Neal Mohan named as her successor. Wojcicki took on an advisory role for Google and parent company Alphabet.[99] Wojcicki died a year and a half later from non-small-cell lung cancer, on August 9, 2024.[100]

📃 Source: https://surgegraph.io/business/who-owns-51-of-google
Title: The REAL Power: Who Owns 51% of Google?
Content: Alphabet Inc.’s ownership structure, characterized by multiple classes of shares, has become an integral part of the discussion on corporate governance. The Class B shares carry super-voting rights. This structure allows insiders like Google founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin to avoid accountability for important company decisions. Grasping these dynamics is key to understanding how and why important strategic decisions are made within the company.
Larry Page and Sergey Brin have significant power as special individual shareholders. Their joint ownership of Class B shares further increases their power. Their dominance over voting rights gives them the power to dictate the company’s actions, policies, and long-term trajectory. This shows just how much a single shareholder—here, by far the biggest—can shape corporate strategy.

Source: https://surgegraph.io/business/who-currently-owns-alphabet
Title: Who Currently Owns Alphabet? A Deep Dive Investigation
Content: With that, Alphabet Inc. took its place as one of the world’s most powerful companies. Its unusual ownership structure and the dreams of its founders have been integral to its success. Larry Page and Sergey Brin maintain extreme control through their Class B shares. This helps to keep the company focused on delivering against its long-term goal. Alphabet’s growth is primarily driven by institutional investors and individual shareholders. Their trust and interest are a testament to the larger market’s confidence in the company.
Understanding who currently owns Alphabet helps us understand how decisions are made. This realization goes a long way in explaining why their business model is thriving. Alphabet’s influence is expected to increase, determining the future of tech, business, and innovation.

Title: Who Really Owns Google (Alphabet) and Who Controls It - KAMIL FRANEK Business Analytics
Content: #4 Sergey Brin (5.7%)
Sergey Brin owns 5.7% of Alphabet’s shares. However, Sergey Brin controls 24.9% of all votes thanks to owning super-voting shares. As of December 2022, the market value of Sergey Brin’s stake in Alphabet was $65.4 billion.
Sergey Brin owned 739 million shares in Alphabet and controlled 3,687 million shareholder votes as of December 2022.
Sergey Brin cofounded Google with Larry Page in 1998. Thanks to his stake in Alphabet Inc., he is one of the world’s wealthiest people.
Sergey does not currently hold any executive position within Google or Alphabet. However, he is still, together with Larry Page, a controlling shareholder, and member of Alphabet’s Board of Directors.
He was born in Moscow, but his family emigrated to the US when he was six years old. He met with Larry Page at Standford when he needed help with math on its PageRank algorithm. Sergey’s extroversion balanced the lack of it in Larry’s case.

Title: The REAL Power: Who Owns 51% of Google?
Content: goals with shareholder interests.Key Individual Shareholders“Sergey Brin (Left) and Larry Page (Right).” Source: Joi Ito, CC BY 2.0Larry Page and Sergey Brin, Google’s co-founders, met as PhD students at Stanford University, where they conceived the idea for Google. They stand out as the primary individual shareholders. Their substantial holdings of Class B shares afford them extraordinary control over the company’s direction. Collectively, Larry Page and Sergey Brin command 51% of the voting power, enabling them to influence a number of key corporate decisions and policies.Implications of Alphabet’s Unique Power StructureMost public companies operate on the principle of “one share, one vote.” But Alphabet, the parent company of Google, has a unique governance structure that grants a small group of insiders near-absolute control. This control stems from the existence of Class B shares, which hold ten times the voting power of ordinary Class A shares.Larry Page and Sergey Brin,

Source: https://surgegraph.io/business/who-owns-51-of-google
Title: The REAL Power: Who Owns 51% of Google?
Content: Larry Page and Sergey Brin, Alphabet’s co-founders, own the majority of these super-voting shares, giving them an effective 51% control over the company. This unusual arrangement has profound implications for how Alphabet is run, influencing everything from its long-term strategy and investments to its approach to social responsibility.
The Benefits of Concentrated Control
This concentration of power offers several potential advantages. It allows Alphabet to:
Think Long-Term: Freed from the pressure of quarterly earnings reports and activist investors, Page and Brin can prioritize long-term projects and investments, even if they don’t offer immediate returns. This has allowed Alphabet to pursue ambitious moonshots like self-driving cars (Waymo) and life extension research (Calico).

Title: Who Owns YouTube?
Content: Who owns YouTube? As a subsidiary of Google, YouTube operates under the broader umbrella of Alphabet Inc., a multinational conglomerate. Google’s acquisition of YouTube in 2006 enabled the platform to expand and reach unprecedented heights. Since its inception, YouTube has transformed the digital landscape, changing how we consume and share video content. Its user-friendly interface, a vast library of videos, and engagement opportunities have attracted billions of users worldwide. Under Google’s ownership, YouTube benefits from the resources, technology, and expertise of one of the world’s most influential companies. Under Google’s guidance, YouTube has continued to evolve, offering innovative features, expanding its reach, and fostering a vibrant community of creators and viewers. While Google owns YouTube, it’s essential to recognize that YouTube operates as a distinct platform with its dedicated team and infrastructure. This allows YouTube to retain its unique identity and provide

Source: https://businessmodelanalyst.com/who-owns-youtube/?srsltid=AfmBOopvdYBCSmjGoxBTn9ikGpZaHh_oQ_AYTx7sIUKoHYpjemYYzvpo
Title: Who Owns YouTube?
Content: Current Ownership Structure
YouTube is owned by Google, which is a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc.
Alphabet Inc. is a multinational conglomerate created in 2015 as a result of Google’s corporate restructuring. Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin formed Alphabet to allow for autonomy in their various business ventures and to separate Google’s core internet business from its other innovative projects.
Alphabet’s Role In YouTube’s Ownership
As a wholly owned subsidiary of Google, YouTube is part of Alphabet Inc. Alphabet has complete ownership and control of YouTube’s operations, finances, and strategic decisions. Alphabet’s overarching objective is to manage and expand its diverse portfolio of businesses, of which YouTube is a significant component.
Alphabet’s Subsidiaries and Structure

Title: A Complete List of 200+ Companies Acquired by Google
Content: Google. One name, multiple features, and more coming our way. But, how can a company founded just two decades ago come up with a bundle of features? As per websitebuilder.org, Google is currently offering 271 products. Like seriously? How? Well, a significant part of this expansion can be attributed to the companies acquired by Google over the years. The number looks huge.
But what if I tell you that this can only be achieved with strategic partnerships or acquisitions? And Google has done exactly the same. They have acquired many brands sheltered them and presented them as a product of Google. So which are those companies acquired by Google? And how many companies Google has acquired? In this blog, we’ll give you the entire list of those companies acquired by Google.
Let’s have a look at the best 10 companies acquired by Google
